questions.question: What is the primary purpose of distress tolerance skills?

options.text: To eliminate the emotion completely so you never feel it again
To get through a crisis moment safely without making things worse
To suppress your feelings so others do not notice them
To analyze the root cause of the emotion in the moment

questions.explanation: Distress tolerance is not about fixing or eliminating the emotion. It is about getting through the crisis moment safely so you can deal with the situation when you are calmer and thinking more clearly.

questions.question: What does the 'T' in TIPP stand for, and how does it work?

options.text: Thinking -- it redirects your thoughts to positive memories
Temperature -- it activates the mammalian dive response to slow heart rate
Timing -- it teaches you to wait before reacting
Tension -- it increases muscle tension to match your emotional state

questions.explanation: The 'T' stands for Temperature. Applying cold water or ice to your face activates the mammalian dive response, which is hard-wired to slow heart rate when the face is exposed to cold, rapidly calming your body.

questions.question: Why is the exhale made longer than the inhale during Paced Breathing?

options.text: It helps you hold more oxygen in your lungs
It activates the parasympathetic nervous system, signaling that it is safe to calm down
It increases your heart rate to burn off adrenaline
It triggers the fight-or-flight response to help you escape danger

questions.explanation: A longer exhale directly activates your parasympathetic nervous system (your body's 'brake pedal'). This sends a signal to your brain that the danger has passed and it is safe to calm down.

questions.question: How does Paired Muscle Relaxation achieve relaxation?

options.text: By keeping muscles tense for as long as possible to exhaust them
By tensing a muscle group and then suddenly releasing it, creating deeper relaxation than before
By stretching muscles slowly until the tension fades
By massaging each muscle group in sequence

questions.explanation: Paired Muscle Relaxation works on the principle that when you intentionally tense a muscle group and then release it suddenly, the muscle becomes more relaxed than it was before the tension. The contrast between tension and release deepens the relaxation response.

questions.question: When is the TIPP skill most appropriate to use?

options.text: When you are mildly worried about a future event
When you are in a full-blown emotional crisis like a panic attack
When you want to fall asleep more easily at night
When you want to improve your long-term emotional awareness

questions.explanation: The TIPP skill is designed as an emergency intervention for moments when your body's 'gas pedal' is floored -- like during a panic attack or extreme emotional overwhelm. It rapidly changes your body chemistry to bring you out of the crisis zone.

title: Calming the Storm: Crisis Mode and the TIPP Skill - Quiz
